zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Verdun 2nd Baroness de Verdun於1351年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾布利沃斯時，她的父親Edmund de Verdun of Bressingham23歲，母親Maud de Beaumont22歲。她於1377年與Hugh Bradshaw在英國英格蘭藍卡爾布拉可洛德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1436年11月24日在英國藍卡爾皮爾金頓享年85歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Verdun 2nd Baroness de Verdun于1351年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾布利沃斯时，她的父亲Edmund de Verdun of Bressingham23岁，母亲Maud de Beaumont22岁。她于1377年与Hugh Bradshaw在英国英格兰藍卡爾布拉可洛德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1436年11月24日在英国藍卡爾皮爾金頓享年85岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Verdun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Virden
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Pierre, Renaud, Sylvie.
French: habitational name from (Le) Verdon, the name of several places in various parts of France, for example in Dordogne, Gironde, and Marne. Compare   Ferdon  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Verdun, the name of a fief in Vessey (Manche), France. Compare   Verdun  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Verdun 2nd Baroness de Verdun was born in 1351, in Brixworth, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Edmund de Verdun of Bressingham, was 23 and her mother, Maud de Beaumont, was 22. She married Hugh Bradshaw in 1377, in Blackrod, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 24 November 1436, in Pilkington, Lancashire, England, at the age of 85.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Verdun 2nd Baroness de Verdun est née en 1351, à Brixworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Edmund de Verdun of Bressingham, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Maud de Beaumont, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Hugh Bradshaw en 1377, à Blackrod, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 24 novembre 1436, à Pilkington, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 85 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Verdun 2nd Baroness de Verdun 1351 in Brixworth, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund de Verdun of Bressingham 23 und ihre Mutter Maud de Beaumont 22. Sie hat 1377 in Blackrod, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Hugh Bradshaw geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 24. November 1436 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Pilkington, Lancashire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Verdun 2nd Baroness de Verdun è nata il nel 1351 nel Brixworth, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, Edmund de Verdun of Bressingham, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Maud de Beaumont, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Hugh Bradshaw nel 1377, nel Blackrod, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 24 novembre 1436, nel Pilkington, Lancashire, England, all'età di 85 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Verdun 2nd Baroness de Verdunが1351年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブリクスウオルスで生まれた時，父Edmund de Verdun of Bressinghamは23歳，母Maud de Beaumontは22歳であった。1377年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアブラクロドでHugh Bradshawと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1436年11月24日にイギリスランカシュアイアピルキントンで85歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Verdun 2nd Baroness de Verdun(이)가 1351년 영국 노르섬프토실르 브리스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund de Verdun of Bressingham(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Beaumont(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1377년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 블랙로드에서 Hugh Bradshaw(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1436년 11월 24일 영국 랭카실르 피킨그턴에서 85세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Verdun 2nd Baroness de Verdun nasceu em 1351, em Brixworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund de Verdun of Bressingham, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Beaumont, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Hugh Bradshaw em 1377, em Blackrod, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 24 de novembro de 1436, em Pilkington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 85 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Verdun 2nd Baroness de Verdun родилась в 1351 г. в местности бриксворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund de Verdun of Bressingham было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Maud de Beaumont, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh Bradshaw в 1377 г. в местности блэккрод, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 24 ноября 1436 г. в местности пилкингтон, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 85 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Verdun 2nd Baroness de Verdun nació en 1351, en Brixworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund de Verdun of Bressingham, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Maud de Beaumont, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Hugh Bradshaw en 1377, en Blackrod, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 24 de noviembre de 1436, en Pilkington, Lancashire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 85 años.
